title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 60.
content:
There was an Old Man of the South,
    Who had an immoderate mouth;
    But in swallowing a dish,
    That was quite full of fish,
    He was choked, that Old Man of the South.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 60.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
